title: crusea tricocca
intro: Crusea tricocca, also known as Marsh St. Johns Wort or Marsh Cudweed, is a perennial herb native to wetlands in Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and antimicrobial effects, providing various health benefits including reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Crusea tricocca can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is sometimes used topically as a cream or salve to promote wound healing and skin health.
